title: Supertabs | Here Enterprise Browser
description: In a typical web browser, you can open multiple web pages in one window, and then switch between web pages by selecting tabs.
text: Get started Supertabs On this page Supertabs In a typical web browser, you can open multiple web pages in one window, and then switch between web pages by selecting tabs. In the HERE browser, you can open multiple web pages and arrange them in columns or rows so that you can view their contents simultaneously. A collection of tabs is called a supertab. You can create a supertab by dragging a tab onto another tab. The new supertab contains the two tabs. You can then add or remove app or website tabs, arrange them in any combination of rows and columns, and resize them to suit your needs and your workflow. info With desktop HERE Enterprise Browser, you can also snap together HERE browser windows (containing supertabs) and desktop application windows (for example, Microsoft Excel). See Snap for more information. This capability is not available in HERE Zero. If the apps or websites that the tabs display are configured to support Signals, you can change or select data in one tab and the data is updated across all tabs in the same supertab. For example, you might have apps for Customer Account Details, Customer History, and Current Order, each of which contains a Customer ID. If you choose a different customer, all the apps update to display the new customer's data. Pre-defined supertabs might be provided to you by your organization, and available via search. Modify supertabs​ If you have permission to modify a supertab, you can do the following with desktop HERE Enterprise Browser (but not Here Zero): Save the supertab, and later find it in a list of saved supertabs to reopen. In addition to preserving the tab content, saved supertabs keep the arrangement of tabs as it was when you saved it. Rename the supertab. Drag a tab from one supertab into a different supertab, or make it a top-level tab by itself. Zoom supertabs​ When viewing a supertab, you can use keyboard shortcuts to zoom in and out, similarly to zooming tabs in a typical web browser. When you zoom a tab, the action affects only the content of the tab; when you zoom a supertab, the action affects the content of all the tabs in that supertab. All tabs in the supertab are adjusted based on their current zoom state. For example, if a particular tab is already zoomed in, it bumps to the next level from where it is, not to the same level as others that started at 100%. The keyboard shortcuts are similar to the shortcuts for zooming tabs: add the Shift key to apply zoom to the whole supertab. Action Microsoft Windows macOS Zoom in on a tab Control++ Command++ Zoom in on a supertab Control+Shift++ Command+Shift++ Zoom out on a tab Control+- Command+- Zoom out on a supertab Control+Shift+- Command+Shift+- Zooming is not continuous: each time you zoom in or out, the zoom level adjusts by a set amount. info If you are an admin with the appropriate permissions, you can publish a supertab for users to work with. Publishing a supertab does not make it immediately available to others. Instead, an admin manages access to the supertab and then activates it for the assigned users. See Manage supertabs. In the HERE browser, you can open multiple web pages and arrange them in columns or rows so that you can view their contents simultaneously. A collection of tabs is called a supertab. You can create a supertab by dragging a tab onto another tab. The new supertab contains the two tabs. You can then add or remove app or website tabs, arrange them in any combination of rows and columns, and resize them to suit your needs and your workflow. info With desktop HERE Enterprise Browser, you can also snap together HERE browser windows (containing supertabs) and desktop application windows (for example, Microsoft Excel). See Snap for more information. This capability is not available in HERE Zero. If the apps or websites that the tabs display are configured to support Signals, you can change or select data in one tab and the data is updated across all tabs in the same supertab. For example, you might have apps for Customer Account Details, Customer History, and Current Order, each of which contains a Customer ID. If you choose a different customer, all the apps update to display the new customer's data. Pre-defined supertabs might be provided to you by your organization, and available via search. Drag a tab from one supertab into a different supertab, or make it a top-level tab by itself. When you zoom a tab, the action affects only the content of the tab; when you zoom a supertab, the action affects the content of all the tabs in that supertab. title: HERE Notifications | Here Enterprise Browser
description: Web apps that are added by admins can send HERE notifications.
text: Get started HERE Notifications On this page HERE Notifications Web apps that are added by admins can send HERE notifications. This page explains how to work with notifications you receive from web apps. For details about creating notifications, see Add notifications. HERE notifications and the HERE Notification Center are not available in HERE Zero. On the desktop​ You can receive and respond to HERE notifications on your desktop, as with other notification services like those of your operating system or web browser. Unlike other notification services, these notifications can be created to stay visible on the desktop until you interact with them. In the HERE Enterprise Browser application​ A red dot on the notification icon in the browser indicates that you have unread notifications. Select the icon to go to the Notification Center. In the Dock​ If the Dock component is enabled in your environment, it shows a red dot when you have unread notifications. Select the icon to go to the Notification Center. In HERE Notification Center​ You can view your notifications in the separate Notification Center application. Depending on how the notifications are configured, you can sort by different attributes, set reminders, clear or dismiss notifications, and more. Notifications that are dismissed on the desktop persist in the Notification Center application until you clear them. Respond to notifications​ Notifications can provide simple information, or they can request complex responses to calls to action. They can include extensive forms that let applications process the data that you provide in the form fields. They can use different banner colors to indicate different categories or priorities. Some notifications let you set a reminder. This feature lets you be reminded of the notification at a more convenient time. The notification re-appears at the time that you select. Table of Contents On the desktop In the HERE Enterprise Browser application In the Dock In HERE Notification Center Respond to notifications Get started HERE Notifications On this page HERE Notifications Web apps that are added by admins can send HERE notifications. This page explains how to work with notifications you receive from web apps. For details about creating notifications, see Add notifications. HERE notifications and the HERE Notification Center are not available in HERE Zero. On the desktop​ You can receive and respond to HERE notifications on your desktop, as with other notification services like those of your operating system or web browser. Unlike other notification services, these notifications can be created to stay visible on the desktop until you interact with them. In the HERE Enterprise Browser application​ A red dot on the notification icon in the browser indicates that you have unread notifications. Select the icon to go to the Notification Center. In the Dock​ If the Dock component is enabled in your environment, it shows a red dot when you have unread notifications. Select the icon to go to the Notification Center. In HERE Notification Center​ You can view your notifications in the separate Notification Center application. Depending on how the notifications are configured, you can sort by different attributes, set reminders, clear or dismiss notifications, and more. Notifications that are dismissed on the desktop persist in the Notification Center application until you clear them. Respond to notifications​ Notifications can provide simple information, or they can request complex responses to calls to action. They can include extensive forms that let applications process the data that you provide in the form fields. They can use different banner colors to indicate different categories or priorities. Some notifications let you set a reminder. This feature lets you be reminded of the notification at a more convenient time. The notification re-appears at the time that you select.

title: Content protection | Here Enterprise Browser
description: HERE enables companies to safeguard valuable data by restricting actions that users might take that could lead to data leakage.
text: Get started Content protection On this page Content protection HERE enables companies to safeguard valuable data by restricting actions that users might take that could lead to data leakage. Protections are defined based on the internet domain of the content. This strategy enables apps containing confidential or proprietary data to run alongside general-purpose apps while minimizing the risk of that data escaping. Content protection features can form a part of a company's broader strategy for data loss prevention (DLP). The following operations can be restricted on a per-domain basis: Screen capture via screenshots or screen-sharing software Printing Copying and pasting; pasting of protected data can be allowed into specified apps or not at all. Dragging and dropping blocked content from a HERE application to any other application. info Content protection settings cannot be enforced in HERE Zero. Screen capture​ When content for a domain is protected from screen capture, the window containing that content is blocked from appearing in screenshots or screen-sharing software. On Microsoft Windows, the blocked window is blacked out; on macOS, the blocked window is invisible. The blocked window appears normal to the user who is attempting the capture. Screen capture protection affects the entire window containing the blocked content, even if that content is in only one tab or supertab. Printing​ When content for a domain is protected from printing, the Print context menu is disabled, and other methods for printing produce no output. Copy and paste​ Copy and paste operations can be prevented or protected, based on the domain of the copied content or the pasted content. Administrators can completely block content from being copied, or they can allow it to be copied only to a Secure Clipboard. Correspondingly, they can specify that a domain can receive pasted content only if it is not protected or that a domain can receive any pasted content, including protected content from the Secure Clipboard. If a user tries to paste protected content into an app or site that does not have access to it, an (admin-specified) error message is displayed instead. info Preventing copy and paste does not impact drag and drop within a HERE application. Drag and drop​ Drag-and-drop operations based on the HTML5 drag and drop API can be blocked, based on the domain of the originating content. If drag-and-drop is blocked, the user is unable to drag the content from a HERE application to any other application, including other HERE applications. Drag-and-drop is automatically blocked for a domain when copying is protected or blocked for that domain Applications that depend on HTML5 drag-and-drop might not function properly when dragging is blocked. info Preventing drag-and-drop does not impact Snap. Snap affects windows but not the content contained in them. Table of Contents Screen capture Printing Copy and paste Drag and drop Get started Content protection On this page Content protection HERE enables companies to safeguard valuable data by restricting actions that users might take that could lead to data leakage. Dragging and dropping blocked content from a HERE application to any other application. info Content protection settings cannot be enforced in HERE Zero. On Microsoft Windows, the blocked window is blacked out; on macOS, the blocked window is invisible. The blocked window appears normal to the user who is attempting the capture. If a user tries to paste protected content into an app or site that does not have access to it, an (admin-specified) error message is displayed instead. info Preventing copy and paste does not impact drag and drop within a HERE application. Drag and drop​ Drag-and-drop operations based on the HTML5 drag and drop API can be blocked, based on the domain of the originating content. If drag-and-drop is blocked, the user is unable to drag the content from a HERE application to any other application, including other HERE applications. Drag-and-drop is automatically blocked for a domain when copying is protected or blocked for that domain Applications that depend on HTML5 drag-and-drop might not function properly when dragging is blocked. info Preventing drag-and-drop does not impact Snap. Snap affects windows but not the content contained in them.

title: AI Center | Here Enterprise Browser
description: AI Center is available only with desktop HERE Enterprise Browser. It is not available with HERE Zero.
text: Get started AI Center On this page AI Center info AI Center is available only with desktop HERE Enterprise Browser. It is not available with HERE Zero. HERE AI Center connects to your organization's artificial intelligence (AI) model for a seamless in-browser experience. It extracts the content you are currently viewing and sends it, along with your prompt, as context to your AI model. AI Center then receives your AI model's response and returns it to you. Most enterprise AI solutions require multiple back-end integrations (for example, email or customer relationship management (CRM) systems) to provide additional context. AI Center makes authorized browser content from multiple systems available to your large language model (LLM) through a single integration. Your active content is securely available to your AI model, so you can, for example, prompt "tell me more about this client" while viewing multiple tabs with information related to a given client, such as an email thread, a CRM record, and a proposal draft. AI Center is visible only if it has been configured for your organization. The landscape of AI is constantly evolving. The AI models and providers in the following list are fully supported in HERE Enterprise Browser. If your organization wishes to enable access to a model or use a provider not in this list, contact HERE to discuss options. Provider Models Anthropic Claude 4 Sonnet AWS (Bedrock) Claude 4 Sonnet Google Gemini 2.0 Microsoft Azure GPT-5.0, GPT-4.1 Microsoft Copilot Prometheus OpenAI GPT-5.0, GPT-4.1 AI Center modes​ info Content is what is shown on your active browser tab or supertab. Context is what extracted content becomes when attached to your prompt. Use AI Center in general mode or context mode. General mode allows you to prompt your organization’s AI model, irrespective of the active content on your tab or supertab. Context mode enables you to apply your prompt to the active tab or supertab in your browser. Use AI Center to prompt for content summarization, analysis, and new content generation. info If needed, use the icons underneath the response to copy, retry, shorten, or elaborate on the initial response. You can use these options only once per conversation. Keep your AI Center conversation open while you browse between tabs and supertabs, or start a new conversation. Switching between tabs does not clear a window's conversation. Also, switching between general and context modes does not clear a window's conversation. You must explicitly clear a window's conversation and start a new chat by clicking New Chat. Access AI Center​ Click the AI Center ✦ icon at the top right of your browser window. AI Center's panel opens to the right. If you are a new user to AI Center, you might need to acknowledge the AI Usage Agreement. To continue, click Agree and Continue. If more than one AI model is configured for your organization, you can select which AI model to use. Responses in the chat sessions appear under a heading that corresponds to the model that generated them. You can switch to another model during a chat session while preserving the chat history. General mode​ When you open the panel, you are placed in general mode by default. Click one of the pre-set general prompts supplied at the top of the panel (Tell me, Explain, Describe), or type your own prompt. In this mode, your prompt is sent without contextual information and your organization’s AI model responds based on the data it has available. When you close the panel, and then again open the panel, your prompt and reply are still there. To clear the current AI conversation, click New Chat at the top right of the panel; this applies to general and context modes. Context mode​ To supply a contextual prompt to AI Center, click Context at the bottom of the panel. You see Context change color to indicate that you are now in context mode. Click one of the prompts supplied at the top of the panel, or type your own prompt. info The content displayed in your active tab or supertab must be explicitly enabled for AI participation by your admin. Content that is not explicitly enabled is excluded from your prompt. If no content in your tab or supertab is enabled, Context is grayed out and unavailable for selection. Type your prompt and click the action button to submit. Table of Contents AI Center modes Access AI CenterGeneral mode Context mode Get started AI Center On this page AI Center info AI Center is available only with desktop HERE Enterprise Browser. title: Deep Search | Here Enterprise Browser
description: Deep Search transforms HERE's search functionality into a powerful productivity tool that quickly aggregates information across the enterprise's entire application ecosystem.
text: Get started Deep Search On this page Deep Search Deep Search transforms HERE's search functionality into a powerful productivity tool that quickly aggregates information across the enterprise's entire application ecosystem. Enhanced search capabilities​ You can search not just for web and desktop apps but also inside apps, finding specific data like contacts, documents, incidents, and records from various services. Depending on the configuration of your company's HERE instance, you might be able to search in any of the following applications: Microsoft 365 Microsoft Dynamics CRM ServiceNow Salesforce Atlassian Zendesk Zoom Slack Google Workspace (includes Google Chat) Unified search experience​ You can access data from multiple sources through a single search bar instead of opening each approved app or URL separately. Extensibility​ Organizations or vendors can create custom search agents specific to their needs (like proprietary research, instrument search, custom CRM, or employee directories). Responsive user experience​ Results from different search agents appear as they become available rather than forcing users to wait for all results, making the search feel fast and responsive. Integration flexibility​ Administrators can add and configure supported search agents through the Admin Console to meet their organization's specific needs. Content access control​ Search agents can be assigned to specific user groups, ensuring people only see relevant search results based on their permissions. Seamless content opening​ When users click search results, approved web content opens within the HERE browser rather than an external browser. Content from a desktop app opens in the desktop app. Table of Contents Enhanced search capabilities Unified search experience Extensibility Responsive user experience Integration flexibility Content access control Seamless content opening Get started Deep Search On this page Deep Search Deep Search transforms HERE's search functionality into a powerful productivity tool that quickly aggregates information across the enterprise's entire application ecosystem. Enhanced search capabilities​ You can search not just for web and desktop apps but also inside apps, finding specific data like contacts, documents, incidents, and records from various services. Depending on the configuration of your company's HERE instance, you might be able to search in any of the following applications: Microsoft 365 Microsoft Dynamics CRM ServiceNow Salesforce Atlassian Zendesk Zoom Slack Google Workspace (includes Google Chat) Unified search experience​ You can access data from multiple sources through a single search bar instead of opening each approved app or URL separately. Extensibility​ Organizations or vendors can create custom search agents specific to their needs (like proprietary research, instrument search, custom CRM, or employee directories). Responsive user experience​ Results from different search agents appear as they become available rather than forcing users to wait for all results, making the search feel fast and responsive. Integration flexibility​ Administrators can add and configure supported search agents through the Admin Console to meet their organization's specific needs. Content access control​ Search agents can be assigned to specific user groups, ensuring people only see relevant search results based on their permissions. Seamless content opening​ When users click search results, approved web content opens within the HERE browser rather than an external browser. Content from a desktop app opens in the desktop app.

title: Signals | Here Enterprise Browser
description: Signals are linkages among apps for workflows or sharing data.
text: Get started Signals On this page Signals Signals are linkages among apps for workflows or sharing data. For workflow signals, you can select from available apps to handle an item of data. For data signals, enabled apps in the same supertab are automatically linked to share data, or you can link supertabs and top-level tabs to share data signals. Data signals​ Signals indicate context-sharing, where different apps automatically share linked data. For example, if the linked apps show customer data, and you select a different customer in one app, the linked apps update to show data for that customer. Apps that support data signals automatically share signals with other apps in the same supertab. You can also explicitly link multiple top-level tabs using color channels; top-level tabs might contain a single app or they might be supertabs containing multiple apps. The color appears on the tab header so you can quickly see which top-level tabs are linked. For example, all top-level tabs with a red bar in the header share signals with one another, while those with blue share different signals. To color-code, right-click each top-level tab and choose the same color. You can have different groups of color-coded tabs at the same time. To remove a top-level tab from a color-coded group, right-click it and choose the white box with the slash through it. Workflow signals​ Workflow signals enable you select data from one app and do something with it via another app. For example, you could select a contact in a directory app and then choose a chat app to send a message to that person. Depending on the type of data and the operation, you might need to choose any of the following: The type of operation; for example, call, chat, or email a contact The app to perform the operation; for example, which chat app should send a chat message? The instance of the app to use, if you have it running in more than one tab For example, after clicking a contact named "Owen Grandy" you might see a dialog box like the following to choose how to handle it: Depending on the operation used, the number of applications available to the user as well as currently open applications, the dialog box might be simpler; for example, it might provide only an option to choose an app. Table of Contents Data signals Workflow signals Get started Signals On this page Signals Signals are linkages among apps for workflows or sharing data. Data signals​ Signals indicate context-sharing, where different apps automatically share linked data. For example, if the linked apps show customer data, and you select a different customer in one app, the linked apps update to show data for that customer. Apps that support data signals automatically share signals with other apps in the same supertab. You can also explicitly link multiple top-level tabs using color channels; top-level tabs might contain a single app or they might be supertabs containing multiple apps. The color appears on the tab header so you can quickly see which top-level tabs are linked. For example, all top-level tabs with a red bar in the header share signals with one another, while those with blue share different signals. To color-code, right-click each top-level tab and choose the same color. You can have different groups of color-coded tabs at the same time. To remove a top-level tab from a color-coded group, right-click it and choose the white box with the slash through it. Workflow signals​ Workflow signals enable you select data from one app and do something with it via another app. For example, you could select a contact in a directory app and then choose a chat app to send a message to that person. Depending on the type of data and the operation, you might need to choose any of the following: The type of operation; for example, call, chat, or email a contact The app to perform the operation; for example, which chat app should send a chat message? The instance of the app to use, if you have it running in more than one tab For example, after clicking a contact named "Owen Grandy" you might see a dialog box like the following to choose how to handle it: Depending on the operation used, the number of applications available to the user as well as currently open applications, the dialog box might be simpler; for example, it might provide only an option to choose an app.

title: Dock | Here Enterprise Browser
description: HERE's Dock gives you a single point of access to favorites, contents, bookmarks, notifications, and deep search.
text: Get started Dock On this page Dock HERE's Dock gives you a single point of access to favorites, contents, bookmarks, notifications, and deep search. It streamlines your workflows and enhances your overall user experience. Drag Handle​ When you first open the browser, Dock appears at the bottom of your screen. You can drag it around to place it where you want. Dock's location is saved for you when you restart the browser. If you again move it, the new location is saved. Platform Logo​ This is your platform's logo, typically your organization's logo. If you are responsible for providing the logo, see Customize branding. Content​ The integrated content panel allows you to browse and discover approved content directly from Dock; desktop applications are denoted with arrows on the bottom right of the application's icon. Your administrator creates the root content directory. There's no limit to how many content items are at root level or in nested sub-folders. Dock automatically merges folders with the same name and removes duplicates to keep your content tidy and easy to navigate. Collections​ The Collection icon opens an administrator-defined group of content items either in the user's active browser window or in a new window. When users click the icon, they see a list of the collections they have access to. Selecting one launches all items in that collection according to the configured behavior. Bookmarks​ Easily access your bookmarked content from the bookmarks icon. You can also bookmark content within the integrated content panel. For more information, see Getting started. Notifications​ The notifications icon gives you quick access to the notifications panel. If you have unread notifications, an indicator appears on the icon to alert you. For more information, see HERE notifications. Search​ The search icon gives you instant access to the Deep Search feature. You can search not just for web and desktop apps but also inside apps, finding specific data like contacts, documents, incidents, and records from various services. For more information, see Deep Search. Favorites​ Approved content items in your Favorites directory now appear prominently on Dock for easy, single-click access. Desktop applications in your Favorites directory are denoted on Dock with an arrow on the bottom right corner of the application's icon. You can personalize Dock by adding, removing, and reordering approved content items and bookmarked URLs in your Favorites directory. Your administrator can also do this. You and your administrator cannot rearrange or move the HERE icons to the left of your favorites. Dock Control​ Access the following options from the Dock Control: Always on Top Minimize Reset to Default Quit Platform Table of Contents Drag Handle Platform Logo Content Collections Bookmarks Notifications Search Favorites Dock Control Get started Dock On this page Dock HERE's Dock gives you a single point of access to favorites, contents, bookmarks, notifications, and deep search. Dock automatically merges folders with the same name and removes duplicates to keep your content tidy and easy to navigate. For more information, see HERE notifications. Search​ The search icon gives you instant access to the Deep Search feature. For more information, see Deep Search. Favorites​ Approved content items in your Favorites directory now appear prominently on Dock for easy, single-click access. You and your administrator cannot rearrange or move the HERE icons to the left of your favorites. Dock Control​ Access the following options from the Dock Control: Always on Top Minimize Reset to Default Quit Platform

title: Snap | Here Enterprise Browser
description: Use the HERE Snap feature to group both HERE browser windows and desktop application windows (for example, Microsoft Excel).
text: Get started Snap Snap Use the HERE Snap feature to group both HERE browser windows and desktop application windows (for example, Microsoft Excel). Windows that are grouped together with Snap move and minimize as a single unit. Snap allows you to quickly organize and reorganize your desktop to suit your workflow. You can have multiple groups of snapped windows at the same time. Unsnap windows from one snapped group and snap them to another snapped group. Change the layout of the windows in a snapped group. Resize the individual windows in a snapped group. Snap windows to target windows even if Dock or Notifications partially cover the target. Snap windows to any side of a target window; you can also snap windows to any side of a target window that is within a snapped group. Switch between snapped groups as you would between windows. To use Snap, shift-drag a window to a side of another window or snapped group. An overlay displays to guide you to the desired location. To unsnap a window, shift-drag the window away from the other window or snapped group. Get started Snap Snap Use the HERE Snap feature to group both HERE browser windows and desktop application windows (for example, Microsoft Excel). Windows that are grouped together with Snap move and minimize as a single unit. Snap allows you to quickly organize and reorganize your desktop to suit your workflow. You can have multiple groups of snapped windows at the same time. Unsnap windows from one snapped group and snap them to another snapped group. Change the layout of the windows in a snapped group. Resize the individual windows in a snapped group. Snap windows to target windows even if Dock or Notifications partially cover the target. Snap windows to any side of a target window; you can also snap windows to any side of a target window that is within a snapped group. Switch between snapped groups as you would between windows. To use Snap, shift-drag a window to a side of another window or snapped group. An overlay displays to guide you to the desired location. To unsnap a window, shift-drag the window away from the other window or snapped group.
